Specimen information

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria in urine, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal impairment. Serum globulin is low, but other liver and electrolyte parameters are within normal limits. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for diabetes and renal disease progression.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
